source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Fire, Crane/Other Lifting

actions_taken: Fire extinguished with two 30 pound dry chemical fire extinguishers
Mud logging unit and all associated control systems de-energized
PFI system in shaker room removed from service as precaution
PFI and P-Heater systems taken out of service and removed
event.summary: On May 10, 2018, a fire occurred in the mud pit room of the drillship Transocean Deepwater Asgard while commissioning a Precision Fluid Injection (PFI) System. The fire was caused by incorrect wiring of temperature control modules to the wrong junction box, resulting in false temperature readings that prevented the heater safety shut-off from activating. The heater continued to heat the drilling fluid beyond safe limits, causing the discharge hose to ignite. The fire was detected by flame, heat, and smoke detectors and extinguished with portable fire extinguishers. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Add function test to controller before connecting power to P-Heater
Add warnings and training regarding potential for P-Heater temperatures remaining below desired setting
Require commissioning tests to be done with water
Install puck in thermocouple junction box less susceptible to errors
Change hose material to flame resistant material
Add outlet flow meter to monitor for no flow conditions
Add emergency shut down buttons in any room where heater is installed
Require two people to inspect all connections
Require three tests of all wire segments (open, short, and signal) before commissioning tests

hazards.name: Thermal energy from heated drilling fluid

threats.name: Incorrect wiring of temperature control modules to wrong junction box
False temperature signal from twisted wires in junction box
Heater temperature exceeds safe operating limits
Discharge hose exposed to excessive heat

consequences.name: Damage to discharge hose and equipment
Operational shutdown and system removal from service

controls.name: Over-temperature control module (6050)
controls.barrier_role: Safety shut-off to prevent heater overheating
controls.lod_basis: Automatic temperature monitoring and shut-off at 250°F

supporting_text: the 6050 over temperature module read the real time temperature of the heating elements inside the heater, and the safety shut-off was set to 250 degrees Fahrenheit
the 6050 control module should have been reading 'open' and not reading any temperature

the twisted wires mimicked a thermocouple and both controllers detected a safe temperature (97 degrees F), which allowed them to operate under a false reading and prevented the heater from shutting down

controls.name: Process controller module (6040) temperature monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Monitor and maintain target fluid temperature
controls.lod_basis: Real-time temperature reading of fluid outlet

supporting_text: The 6040 module read the real time temperature of the fluid passing through the outlet of the heater and also set the target temperature of the fluid (140 degrees F)
the temperature reading never changed from 97 degrees F, even after the heater was powered off
Both devices read between 72 and 74 degrees F, which indicated another problem that was yet to be found
The fire activated the flame, heat, and smoke detectors, and a full muster occurred
at approximately 1730 hours flame, heat, and smoke detectors activated in the active mud pit room

controls.name: Portable fire extinguishers
controls.lod_basis: Manual intervention with dry chemical extinguishers

supporting_text: He used two 30 pound dry chemical fire extinguishers to extinguish the fire
The fire was put out with portable fire extinguishers and there were no injuries reported

controls.name: Electrical wiring installation procedure
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct wiring connections during installation
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control for correct equipment installation

supporting_text: The procedure for commissioning the P-Heater did not call for adequate testing of the electrical wires to ensure correctness during installation
The DWL technicians had incorrectly connected the wires into the CH4 Pit junction box in the pit room as directed by the rig's electrician

controls.name: Impedance testing of wire connections
controls.barrier_role: Verify correct wire connections during installation
controls.lod_basis: Multimeter impedance test to confirm correct wires

supporting_text: The DWL technician twisted the wires in the CH4 Pit junction box and tested them for impedance with a multimeter. He saw a reading of zero impedance at the CH4 Pit junction box, so he believed that he had the correct wires
The DWL technician received a false-positive on the impedance test and actually did not have the correct wires selected

controls.name: Thermocouple wiring integrity
controls.barrier_role: Ensure temperature sensor circuits are properly connected
controls.lod_basis: Proper connection of thermocouple leads to control modules

supporting_text: DWL conducted a visual inspection of the wire connections and found one wire (yellow) from the 6050 over-temperature thermocouple was not connected to the black wire from the junction box in the mud pit room

the sensor leads from the 6040 and 6050 control modules ran to the junction box labeled CH4 Pit. Inside the H2S Pit junction box there were two pairs of wires that were twisted together (blue to black and red to white) and these wires provided a false signal

controls.name: Junction box labeling and identification
controls.barrier_role: Prevent connection to incorrect junction box
controls.lod_basis: Clear labeling of H2S Pit and CH4 Pit junction boxes

supporting_text: Both boxes were located in the mud pit room, and during the installation of the PFI system in the mud pits, the DWL technician twisted the wires in the CH4 Pit junction box
The system should have been connected into the H2S Pit box

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
